text:
This order was made at a hearing without full notice having been given to the respondent. The reason why the order was made without full notice having been given to the respondent was [insert].

Findings of the court

(for example)

[[Name of proposed purchasers] have made an offer to purchase [the family home] / [other property name] for £[amount].]

[The court is satisfied that this price appears to be the best price reasonably obtainable on a sale of the property.]

[The court is further satisfied that the relevant conveyancing documents, including the contract for sale, were presented to the respondent by [name of conveyancing solicitors] on [date] along with a request that they were returned to them by the respondent. These documents have never been returned to [name of conveyancing solicitors] by the respondent.]

[The respondent has neglected or refused to comply with the order of [date] to sign and execute the relevant conveyancing documents.]

Undertakings to the court

[Insert any undertakings given]

IT IS ORDERED THAT:

[The respondent shall [by [date and time]] / [forthwith] sign [and] / [or] execute and return to [the applicant] / [the conveyancing solicitors] / [etc.] [insert documents] / [all documents necessary to effect [(e.g.) the sale of the property to the proposed purchasers]].]

In default of the respondent having signed [and] / [or] executed [insert documents] / [all documents necessary to effect [(e.g.) the sale of the property to the proposed purchasers]] [by [date and time]] / [within 7 days of the date of this order], the applicant may make a without notice application for the documents to be signed [and] / [or] executed by a judge of the Family Court sitting at [court name] on behalf of the respondent in accordance with section 39 of the Senior Courts Act 1981.

Costs

metadata.source: Standard Family Orders — Volume 1: Financial Remedies (May 2026)
